description: §51.0.J E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-WRITE via a markup event-handler. A derived engine (`<engine for=Health derived=match @marioState {...}>`) auto-declares a read-only `@health`; a direct write in `<button onclick=${ @health = .AtRisk }>` is rejected. The write-side rejection walker previously never descended into markup event-handler attribute expressions, so this compiled clean and emitted corrupting client JS.
rationale: §51.0.J: direct writes to a derived engine's auto-declared variable are E-DERIVED-ENGINE-NO-WRITE, regardless of locus — including a markup event-handler attribute. Not E-DERIVED-WRITE (§6.6.8 governs `const <name>` cells, not engine variables).
